pr.title: Split lbcollision code out properly based on sdata usage
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Reorganized lb assembly object boundaries around lbcollision by moving code and its small-data constants into the objects that actually use them. The old asm/melee/lb/code_80008D30.s file was removed, asm/melee/lb/code_80005BB0.s was renamed to asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s and expanded with the 0x80008D30-era collision routines plus their .sdata/.sdata2 labels, a new asm/melee/lb/code_8000AD8C.s object was added for the following address range, and root asm/sdata.s and asm/sdata2.s lost the labels now owned by those lb objects. obj_files.mk was updated so the build uses lbcollision.o and code_8000AD8C.o instead of code_80005BB0.o and code_80008D30.o.

key_files.role: Renamed from code_80005BB0.s and expanded with moved lbcollision-related routines and their local .sdata/.sdata2 constants.
Deleted after its contents were redistributed into better object boundaries.
New address-named assembly object for the 0x8000AD8C-0x8000B1C8 range, including routines such as func_8000AD8C and related data.
Received local .sdata labels lbl_804D3710 and lbl_804D3718 for jobj assertion/string data.
Removed lb-owned small-data labels lbl_804D36A0 through lbl_804D3718 from the global sdata bucket.
Removed lb-owned floating-point constants lbl_804D79F0 through lbl_804D7A54 from the global sdata2 bucket.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES to build lbcollision.o and code_8000AD8C.o with the new split.

what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/lb/code_80008D30.s, which had contained 3048 lines of assembly beginning at func_80008D30.
Renamed asm/melee/lb/code_80005BB0.s to asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s and appended the moved func_80008D30-and-later collision code into that object.
Added asm/melee/lb/code_8000AD8C.s as a new assembly object for the next address range, starting with func_8000AD8C, func_8000AD98, func_8000ADB4, and similar small state/query helpers.
Moved small-data color/string labels such as lbl_804D36A0 through lbl_804D3708 into lbcollision.s, and lbl_804D3710/lbl_804D3718 into code_8000B1CC.s.
Moved floating-point constants such as lbl_804D79F0 through lbl_804D7A40 into lbcollision.s and lbl_804D7A48/lbl_804D7A50/lbl_804D7A54 into code_8000AD8C.s.
Removed those relocated labels from the monolithic root asm/sdata.s and asm/sdata2.s files.
Updated obj_files.mk by replacing code_80005BB0.o and code_80008D30.o with lbcollision.o and code_8000AD8C.o.
smart_moves: Used sdata/sdata2 usage as evidence for object boundaries instead of splitting purely by address or file-size convenience.
Kept small-data labels in the same assembly object as the code that references them, which is important for reconstructing original object layout and future C decompilation.
Renamed an address-based file, code_80005BB0.s, to the subsystem name lbcollision.s once enough context existed to identify the code.
Preserved an address-based filename, code_8000AD8C.s, for code that was split out but apparently not yet given a stronger subsystem name in the available evidence.
Adjusted obj_files.mk in the same PR as the assembly/data move, avoiding a source-tree reorganization without matching build-order changes.
decomp_lessons: For Melee decomp, .sdata and .sdata2 references are useful fingerprints for original object-file boundaries; use them to decide where assembly chunks belong.
Do not leave object-local constants in global asm/sdata.s or asm/sdata2.s once their owning object has been identified; moving them into the object can make later matching and C migration cleaner.
When renaming an assembly file to a semantic subsystem name, keep unresolved adjacent code in address-based files unless there is direct evidence for a better name.
Object splits must be reflected in obj_files.mk, and the replacement object list should preserve the intended link order around neighboring lb objects.
A mechanical-looking assembly move can still be decompilation-enabling because it establishes correct data ownership before functions are converted to C.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem names such as lbcollision.s when the assembly chunk is identified well enough.
Continue using address-based placeholders such as code_8000AD8C.s for unidentified or only partially classified address ranges.
Preserve existing decomp placeholder symbols like func_80008D30 and lbl_804D79F0 when the PR is reorganizing ownership rather than naming functions or data.
Small-data labels remain named by address-style lbl_804Dxxxx placeholders until stronger semantic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move .section .sdata and .section .sdata2 blocks into the same asm file as their users to model original object-file local small-data layout.
Use sda21 references, such as lbl_804D7A48@sda21(r2) and lbl_804D3EE0@sda21(r13), as clues when grouping assembly and constants.
Split one large address-range file into a semantic lbcollision object plus a new address-based tail object rather than keeping all functions in one catch-all file.
Remove duplicated/global small-data definitions after relocating them, so the link uses the object-local definitions instead of root sdata buckets.
Update TEXT_O_FILES atomically with file renames/splits so the linker sees the new object names.

review_feedback.note: The PR dump contains no issue comments, review comments, or review bodies, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s functions to C after this object-boundary cleanup.
Search for uses or renames of func_80008D30, func_80008DA4, and func_80008FC8 to see how the lbcollision API was eventually named.
Check whether code_8000AD8C.s later received a semantic filename or was absorbed into another lb source file.
Audit other global asm/sdata.s and asm/sdata2.s labels for similar object-local relocation opportunities.
Compare obj_files.mk ordering before and after adjacent lb splits to confirm link-order assumptions.
